name: sourcegraph
description: Sourcegraph — search, browse, stream, prompts, notebooks, ownership, insights, monitors, batch changes, auth, RBAC, webhooks, and audit.

keyword_search.description: Search code across all indexed repositories using Sourcegraph's keyword search. Supports standard Sourcegraph query syntax including repo:, file:, lang: filters.

query.description: Sourcegraph search query (e.g. 'repo:myorg/ lang:go func main')

count.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 30)

format.description: Output format: text, json, or raw (default: text). raw returns full GraphQL response.

read_file.description: Read the contents of a file from a repository at a specific revision.

path.description: Path to the file within the repository

format.description: Output format: text or raw (default: text). raw returns full GraphQL response.

list_repos.description: List repositories indexed by the Sourcegraph instance, optionally filtered by a query string.

count.description: Maximum number of repositories to return (default: 25)

format.description: Output format: text or raw (default: text). raw returns full GraphQL response.

list_files.description: List files and directories at a given path within a repository.

path.description: Path within the repository to list (default: root)

format.description: Output format: text or raw (default: text). raw returns full GraphQL response.

symbols.description: Search for code symbols (functions, classes, variables) across repositories.

kind.description: Symbol kind filter (e.g. function, class, variable)

commit_search.description: Search commits by message, author, content, files, and date ranges using Sourcegraph type:commit search.

query.description: Search terms for commit messages or content

author.description: Filter by commit author name or email

after.description: Only commits after this date (ISO8601, e.g. 2024-01-01)

before.description: Only commits before this date (ISO8601, e.g. 2024-12-31)

format.description: Output format: text, json, or raw (default: text). raw returns full GraphQL response.

diff_search.description: Search actual code changes (diffs) for patterns across repositories using Sourcegraph type:diff search.

query.description: Search pattern to find in code diffs

format.description: Output format: text, json, or raw (default: text). raw returns full GraphQL response.

compare_revisions.description: Compare changes between two revisions in a repository, showing file diffs and stats.

base.description: Base revision (e.g. a commit SHA, branch, or tag)

head.description: Head revision to compare against base

format.description: Output format: text, json, or raw (default: text). raw returns full GraphQL response.

get_contributor_repos.description: Find repositories where a specific contributor has made commits.

author.description: Author name or email to search for

count.description: Maximum number of commits to search through (default: 50)

health.description: Check connectivity to the Sourcegraph instance and verify authentication.

stream_search.description: Execute a streaming search query. Collects Server-Sent Events from Sourcegraph's stream API and returns aggregated results. Faster than GraphQL for large result sets.

query.description: Sourcegraph search query (same syntax as keyword_search)

patternType.description: Pattern type: literal, regexp, structural, keyword (default: keyword)

count.description: Maximum number of results to collect (default: 50, max: 500)

format.description: Output format: text or json (default: text)

stream_search_stats.description: Get search statistics (match count, repo count, duration) without returning full results. Useful for gauging query scope before running full search.

stream_health.description: Check connectivity to Sourcegraph stream API endpoint.

prompts_list.description: List and search prompts in the Prompt Library. Returns prompt name, owner, visibility, and whether it is a draft.

query.description: Filter prompts by name or description substring

owner.description: Filter by owner (user or org namespace, e.g. 'users/alice' or 'orgs/myteam')

first.description: Maximum number of prompts to return (default: 25)

after.description: Cursor for pagination (from previous response's endCursor)

prompts_get.description: Get a single prompt by ID, including its full definition text.

prompts_create.description: Create a new prompt in the Prompt Library.

name.description: Prompt name (must be unique within the owner namespace)

description.description: Human-readable description of what the prompt does

draft.description: Whether the prompt is a draft: true or false (default: false)

prompts_update.description: Update an existing prompt's metadata or definition text.

name.description: New name for the prompt

draft.description: Whether the prompt is a draft: true or false

prompts_delete.description: Delete a prompt from the Prompt Library.

prompts_transfer_ownership.description: Transfer a prompt to a different user or organization.

newOwner.description: New owner namespace ID (user or org)

prompts_health.description: Check connectivity to Sourcegraph and verify prompt API access.

notebooks_list.description: List and search notebooks. Returns title, creator, visibility, star count, and creation date.

starred.description: If 'true', only show notebooks starred by the current user

first.description: Maximum number of notebooks to return (default: 25)

notebooks_get.description: Get a single notebook by ID, including all blocks (markdown, query, file, symbol).

notebooks_create.description: Create a new notebook with blocks. Blocks can be MARKDOWN, QUERY, FILE, or SYMBOL type.

blocks.description: JSON array of block objects. Each block: {"type":"MARKDOWN","markdownInput":"text"} or {"type":"QUERY","queryInput":"repo:foo lang:go"} or {"type":"FILE","fileInput":{"repositoryName":"...","filePath":"...","revision":"HEAD"}} or {"type":"SYMBOL","symbolInput":{"repositoryName":"...","filePath":"...","revision":"HEAD","symbolName":"..."}}

namespace.description: Namespace ID (user or org) that owns this notebook

public.description: Whether the notebook is public: true or false (default: false)

notebooks_update.description: Update an existing notebook's title, blocks, or visibility.

blocks.description: New blocks JSON array (replaces all existing blocks)

notebooks_add_star.description: Star a notebook to bookmark it.

notebooks_health.description: Check connectivity to Sourcegraph and verify notebook API access.

auth_list_auth_providers.description: List all configured authentication providers (builtin, SAML, OIDC, OAuth, HTTP header).

auth_get_auth_provider.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific auth provider.

serviceType.description: Provider service type: builtin, saml, openidconnect, http-header, github, gitlab

auth_list_users.description: List Sourcegraph users with optional search filter.

query.description: Filter users by username or email substring

first.description: Maximum number of users to return (default: 25)

auth_get_user.description: Get detailed information about a specific user.

auth_create_user.description: Create a new Sourcegraph user account. Requires site admin.

email.description: Email address for the new user

auth_delete_user.description: Delete a Sourcegraph user account. Requires site admin.

hard.description: If 'true', hard-delete (purge all data). Default is soft delete.

auth_set_site_admin.description: Promote or demote a user's site admin status. Requires site admin.

admin.description: Set to 'true' to promote, 'false' to demote

auth_get_site_config_auth.description: Read the authentication section of the site configuration. Requires site admin.

auth_update_site_config_auth.description: Update the authentication section of the site configuration. DANGER: changes take effect immediately. Requires site admin.

config.description: JSON object with auth config fields to update (merged with existing config)

auth_health.description: Check connectivity and verify site admin access.

rbac_list_roles.description: List all roles defined in the Sourcegraph instance.

rbac_get_role.description: Get a role's details including its assigned permissions.

rbac_create_role.description: Create a new role. Requires site admin.

name.description: Role name

permissions.description: Comma-separated list of permission IDs to assign

rbac_delete_role.description: Delete a role. Requires site admin.

rbac_assign_role.description: Assign a role to a user. Requires site admin.

username.description: Username to assign the role to

rbac_revoke_role.description: Revoke a role from a user. Requires site admin.

username.description: Username to revoke the role from

rbac_list_permissions.description: List all available permissions that can be assigned to roles.

rbac_get_repo_permissions.description: Get permission information for a specific repository.

rbac_sync_permissions.description: Trigger a permissions sync. Can sync all users or a specific user. Requires site admin.

username.description: Specific user to sync (omit for full sync)

rbac_get_sync_status.description: Get the current status of the permissions sync job.

rbac_health.description: Check connectivity and verify RBAC API access.

ext_svc_list.description: List all configured external service connections.

kind.description: Filter by kind: GITHUB, GITLAB, BITBUCKETCLOUD, BITBUCKETSERVER, GITOLITE, PERFORCE, GERRIT, AZUREDEVOPS, OTHER

ext_svc_get.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific external service. Secrets in the config are redacted.

ext_svc_create.description: Add a new external service connection. Requires site admin.

kind.description: Service kind: GITHUB, GITLAB, BITBUCKETCLOUD, BITBUCKETSERVER, GITOLITE, PERFORCE, GERRIT, AZUREDEVOPS, OTHER

config.description: JSON configuration string (varies by kind — see Sourcegraph docs for schema)

ext_svc_update.description: Update an existing external service connection. Requires site admin.

ext_svc_delete.description: Remove an external service connection. Requires site admin.

id.description: External service node ID to delete

ext_svc_sync.description: Trigger a repository sync for an external service. Requires site admin.

id.description: External service node ID to sync

ext_svc_check_connection.description: Test connectivity and authentication for an external service.

id.description: External service node ID to test

ext_svc_health.description: Check connectivity and verify external service API access.

first.description: Maximum number of webhooks to return (default: 25)

webhooks_get.description: Get detailed information about a specific webhook.

webhooks_create.description: Create a new webhook. Requires site admin.

name.description: Webhook name

codeHostKind.description: Code host kind: GITHUB, GITLAB, BITBUCKETCLOUD, BITBUCKETSERVER

webhooks_update.description: Update an existing webhook. Requires site admin.

name.description: New name

webhooks_delete.description: Delete a webhook. Requires site admin.

webhooks_list_logs.description: List delivery logs for a webhook. Shows request/response status for recent deliveries.

webhooks_get_log.description: Get detailed delivery log including full request and response bodies.

webhooks_health.description: Check connectivity and verify webhook API access.

audit_user_events.description: List event logs for a specific user. Events include searches, page views, feature usage, and admin actions.

first.description: Maximum number of events to return (default: 50)

audit_all_user_events.description: Aggregate event logs across all users. Iterates users and collects recent events. Site admin required.

maxUsers.description: Maximum number of users to query (default: 50)

audit_outbound_requests.description: List outbound HTTP requests made by the Sourcegraph instance (e.g. to code hosts, auth providers). Site admin required.

first.description: Maximum number of requests to return (default: 50)

audit_slow_requests.description: List recently captured slow GraphQL requests. Useful for performance monitoring. Site admin required.

audit_health.description: Check connectivity and verify event log API access.

owners_sync.description: Fetch and parse CODEOWNERS files from all indexed repos (or a specific repo)

owners_get.description: Find owners for a file path in a repo (last matching CODEOWNERS rule wins)

path.description: File path to check ownership for

owners_create.description: Create/upload a CODEOWNERS file for a repo — parses content, validates format, and stores rules in SQLite

file.description: Path to a local CODEOWNERS file to upload

content.description: CODEOWNERS content as a string (used if --file is not provided)

owners_update.description: Update an existing CODEOWNERS file for a repo — replaces all existing rules with new content

file.description: Path to the updated CODEOWNERS file

content.description: Updated CODEOWNERS content as a string (used if --file is not provided)

owners_delete.description: Delete a CODEOWNERS file entry and all its rules for a repo from the local database

repo.description: Repository name to remove from the database

owners_search.description: Find all patterns and repos owned by a person or team

repo.description: Limit search to a specific repo

owners_list_repos.description: List repos that have CODEOWNERS files with rule counts and last-synced times

owners_list_owners.description: List all known owners across all repos with rule counts

owners_unowned.description: Find files with no CODEOWNERS coverage in a repo

repo.description: Repository name to check for unowned files

owners_health.description: Check Sourcegraph connectivity and database state

insights_create_insight.description: Create a new insight (tracked metric) — aligns with createLineChartSearchInsight

name.description: Insight name (e.g. "deprecated-api-usage")

interval.description: Collection interval in hours (default: 24, informational — actual scheduling is external)

insights_update_insight.description: Update an insight's query, description, or interval — aligns with updateLineChartSearchInsight

name.description: Name of the insight to update

insights_collect.description: Run a collection for a specific insight or all insights

name.description: Specific insight to collect (if omitted, collects all)

insights_series_points.description: Show the time-series data points for an insight with ASCII sparkline chart — aligns with InsightsSeries.points

name.description: Name of the insight

format.description: Output format: text or json (default: text)

insights_compare.description: Compare two insights side by side

insights_live_preview.description: Get current values for all insights without storing results — aligns with searchInsightLivePreview

insights_list_views.description: List all insight views with their status — aligns with insightViews

insights_delete_view.description: Delete an insight view and all its collected data — aligns with deleteInsightView

name.description: Name of the insight to delete

insights_validate_query.description: Validate a search query for use in insights — aligns with validateScopedInsightQuery

insights_create_dashboard.description: Create a named dashboard grouping insights — aligns with createInsightsDashboard

name.description: Dashboard name

insights_list_dashboards.description: List all dashboards — aligns with insightsDashboards

insights_add_to_dashboard.description: Add an insight view to a dashboard — aligns with addInsightViewToDashboard

dashboard.description: Dashboard name to add the insight to

insights_remove_from_dashboard.description: Remove an insight view from a dashboard — aligns with removeInsightViewFromDashboard

dashboard.description: Dashboard name to remove the insight from

insights_health.description: Check Sourcegraph connectivity, Vault credentials, and database state

monitor_create.description: Create a new code monitor (maps to createCodeMonitor)

name.description: Monitor name (unique identifier)

query.description: Sourcegraph search query (should include type:diff or type:commit)

webhook.description: Webhook URL to POST alerts to (e.g., Slack incoming webhook)

interval.description: Check interval in minutes (default: 15, informational only)

monitor_update.description: Update a monitor's query, webhook, or interval (maps to updateCodeMonitor)

name.description: Name of the monitor to update

monitor_delete.description: Delete a monitor and its event history (maps to deleteCodeMonitor)

name.description: Name of the monitor to delete

monitor_toggle.description: Enable or disable a monitor (maps to toggleCodeMonitor)

name.description: Name of the monitor to toggle

enabled.description: Set to 'true' to enable or 'false' to disable

monitor_trigger_check.description: Evaluate trigger query for a specific monitor (maps to trigger evaluation concept)

name.description: Specific monitor to check (if omitted, checks all)

since.description: Override 'since' time in ISO8601 format (default: last check time from DB)

monitor_trigger_check_all.description: Evaluate trigger queries for all enabled monitors

monitor_list.description: List all monitors with their status (via currentUser.monitors pattern)

monitor_events.description: Show recent trigger events for a monitor (maps to MonitorTriggerEventConnection)

name.description: Name of the monitor

limit.description: Maximum number of events to show (default: 20)

monitor_test_action.description: Send a test webhook for a monitor (maps to triggerTestWebhookAction)

name.description: Name of the monitor to test

monitor_reset.description: Reset trigger timestamps to re-evaluate from scratch (maps to resetTriggerQueryTimestamps)

name.description: Name of the monitor to reset

monitor_health.description: Check Sourcegraph connectivity and database state

batch_new.description: Create a new batch change spec (saved locally, not yet executed)

name.description: Batch change name (used as identifier)

description.description: Human-readable description of the batch change

query.description: Sourcegraph search query to find target repos (e.g. "repo:myorg/ file:package.json lodash")

script.description: Shell command to run inside each repo clone (e.g. "sed -i 's/old/new/g' file.txt")

branch.description: Branch name to create in each repo (default: batch/{name})

batch_apply.description: Execute a batch change: clone repos, apply script, commit, push, and open GitLab MRs

name.description: Name of the batch change to execute

dry-run.description: Show what would happen without making any changes

batch_preview.description: Dry-run a batch change: show which repos match and what would happen without making any changes

name.description: Name of the batch change to preview

batch_validate.description: Validate a batch change spec exists and is well-formed (required fields, valid Sourcegraph query)

name.description: Name of the batch change to validate

batch_repositories.description: List repos that would be affected by a batch change without cloning or changing anything

name.description: Name of the batch change whose target repos to list

batch_status.description: Show the status of a batch change (open/merged/closed MRs)

name.description: Name of the batch change to check

batch_health.description: Check connectivity to Sourcegraph and GitLab

go_to_definition.description: Find the definition of a symbol from a usage location. Uses compiler-accurate LSP when available, falls back to AST-based analysis.

symbol.description: Symbol name to find definition for

find_references.description: Find all references to a symbol across the codebase. Uses compiler-accurate LSP when available, falls back to AST-based analysis.

symbol.description: Symbol name to find references for

nls_search.description: Natural language semantic search with linguistic matching. Uses hybrid BM25 + TF-IDF + neural embeddings for flexible search that understands intent, not just exact keywords.

query.description: Natural language search query (e.g. 'error handling for HTTP requests')

repo.description: Limit search to a specific repository

deepsearch.description: Multi-step LLM-driven deep code research. Spawns an AI research loop that searches, reads files, traces code paths, and synthesizes a thorough answer. Use for complex questions that require combining information from multiple sources across repositories.

question.description: A detailed question about the codebase to research

provider.description: LLM provider: zai (GLM-5, default) or openrouter (Qwen3-coder)

deepsearch_read.description: Retrieve a stored deep search conversation by ID. Returns the full question, research steps, and synthesized answer.

identifier.description: Conversation ID returned by deepsearch (or prefix match)

count.description: Number of recent conversations to show (default: 10)
